agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature where customers can view their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have submitted directly contradictory requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency as a competitive differentiator, while Operations demands zero location sharing due to driver retention risk. This is a fundamental business conflict that cannot be resolved by engineering — it requires executive arbitration.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Courier Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"A delivery driver employed or contracted by the platform to transport parcels from origin to destination."},{"term":"Real-time Location","definition":"The courier's GPS coordinates as reported by their device during an active delivery, typically updated every few seconds."},{"term":"Delivery Window","definition":"The time period during which a courier is actively en route to deliver a specific parcel to a customer."},{"term":"Location Granularity","definition":"The level of precision at which a courier's position is exposed — ranging from exact GPS coordinates to coarse zone-level approximation (e.g., 'nearby', 'arriving soon')."},{"term":"Courier Visibility","definition":"The business policy governing what location information about a courier is shared with which parties and under what conditions."},{"term":"Driver Retention","definition":"The operational metric measuring the platform's ability to keep couriers working on the platform over time. Directly affected by working conditions and perceived surveillance."},{"term":"Customer Transparency","definition":"The degree to which end customers are informed about the status and location of their delivery in real time."}]},"business_rules":[],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier is stationary for extended period (e.g., traffic, break) and customer sees no movement","expected_behavior":"Unresolved — depends on which stakeholder's requirement is adopted. Exact GPS would show stalled position, raising customer complaints. Hiding location avoids this but removes transparency."},{"scenario":"Courier deviates from expected route (multi-drop batching)","expected_behavior":"Unresolved — exact GPS would expose route deviations that confuse or frustrate customers. Abstracted status would hide this but reduce transparency."},{"scenario":"Courier is near customer's home but delivering to a neighbor first","expected_behavior":"Unresolved — GPS proximity without delivery creates false expectations. Abstracted ETA avoids this."},{"scenario":"Courier location data used in dispute resolution (e.g., 'delivered but not received')","expected_behavior":"Internal audit and dispute resolution may need precise GPS regardless of what is shown to customers — this is a separate concern from customer-facing visibility."},{"scenario":"Regulatory requirements (GDPR, local labor law) restrict sharing worker location with third parties","expected_behavior":"In many jurisdictions, sharing a worker's exact GPS with customers may require explicit courier consent and may be restricted under labor surveillance laws. This has not been addressed by either team."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"High risk in both directions. Losing driver supply (Operations concern) directly impacts delivery capacity and revenue. Losing customer trust and differentiation (Customer Success concern) impacts acquisition and retention. The cost of driver churn (recruiting, onboarding, training) is typically 3-5x the cost of customer churn in two-sided marketplace logistics.","operational":"Driver retention is an existential operational constraint — without couriers, there are no deliveries. Customer-facing tracking is a feature that can be tuned. The asymmetry matters: you can iterate on tracking UX, but you cannot deliver parcels without drivers.","user":"Customers expect delivery visibility (industry standard set by Uber, DoorDash, Amazon). However, industry is shifting from exact GPS to abstracted ETA-based tracking. Couriers expect reasonable privacy protections as a working condition.","risk":"CRITICAL: Potential labor law and data privacy violations if exact GPS is shared without courier consent. Driver unionization or collective action risk if surveillance concerns are ignored. Competitive risk if tracking experience is materially worse than industry standard.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Delivery visibility directly affects perceived service quality, trust, and willingness to reorder. Customers want confidence their parcel is coming — but research shows ETA confidence matters more than GPS dot-on-map."},{"group":"Couriers / Drivers","impact":"Exact GPS sharing creates real and perceived surveillance. Driver attrition is already occurring. This is the supply side of a two-sided marketplace — losing drivers is harder to recover from than losing customers."},{"group":"Customer Success Team","impact":"Their KPIs around NPS and customer satisfaction are tied to transparency features. They need a tracking solution, but their specific requirement of 'exact GPS at all times' may not be the only way to achieve their goal."},{"group":"Operations Team","impact":"Their KPIs around driver retention and fleet capacity are directly threatened. Their blanket 'never show location' position may also be more extreme than necessary."},{"group":"Legal / Compliance","impact":"Not yet consulted but MUST be. GDPR Article 6 and labor surveillance regulations in many jurisdictions impose constraints on sharing worker location data with third parties. This is not optional."},{"group":"Executive Leadership","impact":"This is a strategic product decision about marketplace positioning that requires executive arbitration. Neither team has the authority to make this call unilaterally."}]},"confidence":{"level":35,"reasoning":"Confidence is very low because the spec contains a direct, unresolvable contradiction between two non-negotiable stakeholder requirements. Neither requirement can be implemented without violating the other. Additionally, the legal/regulatory dimension has not been addressed at all, which could invalidate BOTH positions. No acceptance criteria can be defined until this conflict is resolved at the executive level.","high_confidence_areas":["This is a genuine business contradiction that cannot be resolved by engineering","Driver retention is an existential operational constraint in two-sided logistics marketplaces","Legal/privacy review is mandatory before any location-sharing feature ships","Industry trend is moving toward abstracted tracking (ETA-based) rather than raw GPS"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which stakeholder priority should win — this is an executive decision","Whether a middle-ground solution (e.g., zone-based tracking, ETA-only, courier opt-in) would satisfy both teams","Regulatory constraints in the specific jurisdictions this platform operates in","Actual quantitative impact of GPS tracking on driver churn vs. customer satisfaction"],"assumptions":["Both teams are acting in good faith based on real signals from their respective stakeholders","No executive decision or company policy has already been made on this topic","The platform operates in jurisdictions where GDPR or similar privacy regulations apply","There is no existing courier consent mechanism for location sharing"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires exact GPS visibility to customers at all times. Operations requires zero location visibility to customers at all times. These requirements are mutually exclusive and both teams present them as non-negotiable. This is a strategic business decision, not a technical one.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Couriers","Customer Success Team","Operations Team","Executive Leadership","Legal/Compliance"],"options":["A) Side with Customer Success: show exact GPS. Risk: accelerated driver churn, potential labor law violations, operational collapse of delivery capacity.","B) Side with Operations: show no location. Risk: competitive disadvantage, reduced customer trust, Customer Success KPI regression.","C) Executive-mediated compromise: implement abstracted tracking (ETA countdown, zone-based proximity like 'X stops away', status milestones) that gives customers confidence without exposing exact courier GPS. This is the industry-standard approach (Uber shows car icon on approximate route, not raw GPS).","D) Courier-consent model: let couriers opt in to location sharing with appropriate compensation or incentive, respecting autonomy while enabling the feature for willing participants."],"recommendation":"Option C. Abstracted ETA-based tracking with status milestones (picked up → en route → nearby → arriving) satisfies the customer's actual need (confidence their delivery is coming) without creating the surveillance dynamic that drives courier churn. This is the proven industry pattern. However, this decision MUST be made by executive leadership with input from Legal, not by either requesting team. I recommend an immediate executive alignment session with Customer Success, Operations, Legal, and Product before any engineering work begins."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"Legal and regulatory review has not been conducted. In many jurisdictions, sharing a worker's real-time GPS coordinates with third-party customers may require explicit worker consent and may be restricted under labor surveillance and data protection laws (GDPR Article 6, CCPA, local labor codes). This could make Option A illegal regardless of business preference.","affected_stakeholders":["Legal/Compliance","Couriers","Executive Leadership"],"options":["Engage Legal for a privacy and labor law review before defining requirements","Proceed without legal review and risk regulatory exposure"],"recommendation":"Mandatory legal review before any requirement is finalized. This is not optional."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Direct contradiction between two stakeholder requirements makes it impossible to define correct behavior","business_rule_broken":"Cannot simultaneously show and hide courier location to customers"},{"violation":"Legal/regulatory dimension entirely unaddressed","business_rule_broken":"No privacy impact assessment or labor law review has been conducted for a feature that shares worker GPS data with third parties"}]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature where customers can view their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have submitted directly contradictory requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency as a competitive differentiator, while Operations demands zero location sharing due to driver retention risk. This is a fundamental business conflict that cannot be resolved by engineering — it requires executive arbitration."
